# Mako Programming Language

> Source: https://mako-lang.com
> Full reference for LLMs: https://mako-lang.com/llms-full.txt
> Repository: https://github.com/loreste/mako

## What is Mako?

Mako is a compiled, statically typed programming language with its own unique
syntax and identity. Source files use the `.mko` extension. Compiles to C then
native via clang. No garbage collector. **Version 0.1.1.** 239 tests passing.

**Unique surface:** `fn`, `let`, `on Type {}`, `pack`/`pull`/`export`,
`hold`/`share`/`arena`, `crew`/`kick`/`join`/`fan`, `match`, `switch`/`case`.

Exists to fix real pain (GC/nil/err noise/leaks; lifetimes/traits/async coloring)
without cloning any other language. Dual spellings (`func`, `:=`, `import`) are
compat sugar only. Identity: docs/IDENTITY.md (~92%).

Key properties:
- Mako surface: fn, let, on Type {}, pack/pull/export, hold/share/arena, crew/kick/join/fan
- User generics: fn id[T](x: T) -> T (monomorphization)
- Tuples: (int, int), multi-return with let a, b = f()
- Methods: on Type { fn method(self) -> T { ... } }
- Module system: pack name / pull "path" / pull "path" as name / export fn/struct
- Switch: switch expr { case v: ...; default: ... }
- Parallelism: fan(xs, fn(x) { x * x }) — data parallelism across cores
- Concurrency: crew/kick/join, go f(), channels, select, actor (structured)
- Compound assignments: +=, -=, *=, /=, ++, --
- Parallel assignment: a, b = b, a
- If-expressions: let x = if c { a } else { b }
- If with initializer: if init; cond { }
- No GC: ownership via hold/share, arenas for scoped allocation
- Result/Option types enforced (no null, unused Result is a compile error)
- Checked arithmetic: checked_add/sub/mul return Result[int, string]; would_overflow_add/sub/mul
- Graceful shutdown: install_graceful_shutdown, shutdown_requested, server drain lifecycle
- Distributed tracing: trace_begin/end/id/set/current/log/clear, middleware_trace
- Leak detection: leak_mark/check/scope_enter/exit/assert_scope/bytes_since/report_json
- Const functions: const fn for compile-time evaluation
- Parser recovery: multi-error reporting per file
- Speed: as close to native as possible (-O3 -flto; bench-gate <=1.5x)
- Compiles .mko -> C -> native binary (debug -O0, release -O3 -flto)
- WASM/WASI target support (wasm32-wasip1)
- Batteries-included stdlib: HTTP/2 (DATA auto-split ≤16384), TLS 1.3, bcrypt,
  PBKDF2, SCRAM-SHA-256, signal handling, file-system watch, UDP proxy, JSON,
  SQL, regex, UUID v4/v5/v7, ULID, chan_str_send_take, etc.
- Production (0.1.1): H2 frame-size safe replies; empty-string free safety.

## Installation

```bash
curl -fsSL https://github.com/loreste/mako/releases/latest/download/install-release.sh | bash
```

Or from source:
```bash
make install    # installs to ~/.local/bin/mako
```

## Quick Start

```bash
mako init hello --name hello
cd hello
mako run main.mko
mako build main.mko
```

## Hello World (idiomatic Mako)

```mko
fn main() {
    print("hello from mako")
    print(fib(10))
}

fn fib(n: int) -> int {
    if n <= 1 {
        return n
    }
    return fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2)
}
```

## Canonical Mako Style

```mko
pull "strings"

export struct Point {
    x: int
    y: int
}

on Point {
    fn distance(self) -> int {
        return self.x + self.y
    }
}

fn divmod(a: int, b: int) -> (int, int) {
    return (a / b, a % b)
}

fn main() {
    let p = Point { x: 3, y: 4 }
    print(p.distance())

    let q, r = divmod(17, 5)

    let xs = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    let ys = fan(xs, fn(x) { x * x })

    crew t {
        let job = t.kick(work())
        print(job.join())
    }
}
```

## Core Concepts

- **Types:** int, int8, int32, int64, uint64, float, bool, string, byte, void
- **Composite:** []T (slices), map[K]V, chan[T], Option[T], Result[T, E], (T, U) tuples
- **Declarations:** fn, struct, enum, actor, interface, const, export
- **Generics:** fn id[T](x: T) -> T, Result[T, E], Option[T]
- **Methods:** on T { fn M(self) ... } preferred; dual: free functions T_M
- **Locals:** let / let mut; compound: +=, -=, *=, /=, ++, --
- **Control flow:** if/else, if-expr, if init;cond, while, for/in/range, switch/case, match, break, continue, return, defer
- **Ownership:** hold (move), share (RC), arena (bump allocator)
- **Concurrency:** crew { t.kick(f()); j.join() }, go f(), fan, channels, select
- **Error handling:** Result[T, E], Ok/Err, error("msg"), ?, match
- **Units:** pack name; pull "path" / pull "path" as name; export fn/struct; always qualify (pkg.fn)
- **Testing:** fn TestFoo() { assert_eq(1, 1) } in *_test.mko files

## Build Commands

```bash
mako build file.mko              # debug build
mako build --release file.mko    # optimized -O3 -flto
mako run file.mko                # compile and run
mako test path/                  # run tests
mako fmt file.mko -w             # format in place
mako check file.mko              # typecheck only
mako build --target wasm32-wasip1 file.mko -o out.wasm
```

## Stdlib Categories

Output, Strings, Math, File I/O, HTTP Server/Client, HTTP/2 (frame builders +
auto DATA split), TLS 1.3, JSON, Database (SQLite/Postgres), Crypto (SHA, HMAC,
AES-GCM, ChaCha20, bcrypt, PBKDF2, SCRAM-SHA-256), Encoding (base64, hex, gzip),
Regex, UUID/ULID, Cookies/Sessions/Auth, Channels (incl. string move-send),
Concurrency (mutex, atomics, CMap), Event Loop, Binary Buffer, Game Networking,
Signal Handling, File-System Watch, UDP Proxy, Cloud (consistent hashing, rate
limiting, circuit breaker), Checked Arithmetic, Graceful Shutdown, Distributed
Tracing, Leak Detection, Testing.

## Documentation Links

- Website: https://mako-lang.com
- Language Guide: https://mako-lang.com/docs/guide
- Standard Library: https://mako-lang.com/docs/stdlib
- The Mako Book: https://mako-lang.com/docs/book
- Full LLM Reference: https://mako-lang.com/llms-full.txt
